seed: trust vs suspicion; signal_vs_noise; split_vertical; diagonal_fall; stairs against ash_field; pressure_border; soft_glow_vs_hard_shadow; line; resilience_fragility | abstract variation request 358; target batch count 500
title: Echoing Dissonance
reasoning: This piece conveys the emotional tension between trust and suspicion through the clash of warm and cool colors. The palette of lime and electric blue against deeper blacks introduces a sense of electric energy, hinting at both clarity and interference. The patterns of checker and halo collide, representing resilience and fragility—trust forms a stable checker pattern, while suspicion radiates outward chaotically through halos. The primary collision occurs in the center where these patterns overlap, creating a stark focus point that draws the viewer's eye. The broken pixels add intentional disruptions, reflecting the uncertainty embedded in relationships. The layout coils towards an off-center point, representing the push and pull of emotions, while maintaining approximately 10% negative space for balance, mirroring the tension between openness and guardedness.

layout.name: spiral_pressure
layout.description: Marks coil toward or away from a small off-center point.

composition.name: signal_vs_noise
composition.idea: One readable pulse tries to survive inside static, interference, and false echoes.

referenceExample.title: Repair After Guilt
referenceExample.pattern: dark residue patched by small ivory and gold marks

generationRules: Pick 1 pattern type for emotion A.
Pick 1 pattern type for emotion B.
Pick 1 shape seed as the central object.
Apply 1 to 6 broken pixels to create imperfection.
Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the mood is panic, rage, urgency, euphoria, or pressure.
Use the shape seed as an abstract organizing metaphor, not as a literal icon.
Make the two selected pattern types visibly collide, overlap, interrupt, or trade space.
Prefer 4 to 8 distinct hex colors, with at least one quiet field color and one accent color.
Keep the 8x8 grid readable at thumbnail size by preserving clear large-scale structure.
Let one emotion dominate 55 to 70 percent of the square unless the selected layout demands balance.
Use broken pixels as intentional disruptions, scars, repairs, leaks, or false echoes.
Avoid perfectly even distribution unless the chosen composition is control, serenity, clarity, or certainty.
Give the main collision a location: center, edge, corner, diagonal, border, or channel.
generationSpec.negativeSpaceRule: Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the chosen emotional tone needs dense pressure.
